success_surface: Successfully identified merged cells, properly unmerged them using range iteration, counted unique values across multiple row segments, placed results at target locations, and verified outputs before saving.
effective_behaviors: Multi-tool strategy combining pandas for exploratory analysis and openpyxl for precise cell-level modifications
Proactive detection of merged cells before modification attempts
Corrected API usage after encountering errors by iterating through merged ranges instead of batch operations
Modular function design for repeated data extraction patterns
Pre-save validation checking both data values and structural integrity
Step-by-step verification with explicit print statements before finalization
evidence: Used load_workbook() with openpyxl for merged cell inspection and unmerge_cells() calls
Identified 3 merged cell ranges (H2:O10, H11:O20, H21:P24) before modifications
Recovered from TypeError on ws.unmerge_cells() by iterating list(ws.merged_cells.ranges)
Created get_unique_item_count() function for reusable range processing
Verified H10, H20, H24 values AND merged_cells.ranges status before wb.save()
Printed intermediate counts and verification messages throughout execution

memory_items.title: Merged Cell Handling Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Always detect merged cells before modifying sheets; if unmerge fails, iterate through ranges individually with full range notation
memory_items.generalization: Before any sheet modification, inspect merged_cells.ranges. If batch unmerge causes errors, loop through each range and call unmerge_cells with explicit A1:B2 style coordinates.

memory_items.title: Exploratory Analysis Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for quick schema checks and openpyxl for detailed cell structure before implementing changes
memory_items.generalization: First load with pandas for column names/data types, then load with openpyxl for cell-level inspection including merged cells, formulas, and formatting.

memory_items.title: Pre-Save Verification Checkpoint
memory_items.lesson: Validate both data integrity and structural state (merged cells, row counts) before saving output
memory_items.generalization: Create a verification step that checks target cell values, row/column counts, and merged cell status before calling save().

memory_items.title: Error Recovery from Library API Limitations
memory_items.lesson: When Excel library methods fail, find alternative implementation patterns documented in library references
memory_items.generalization: If an API method throws unexpected errors, search for alternative approaches (e.g., iterating ranges vs. batch operations) rather than forcing the original call.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### 1. Merged Cell Handling Protocol
- Always detect merged cells before modifying sheets using `ws.merged_cells.ranges`
- If `unmerge_cells()` without arguments fails, iterate through each range: `for mr in list(ws.merged_cells.ranges): ws.unmerge_cells(str(mr))`
- After unmerge, verify with `list(ws.merged_cells.ranges)` returning empty list

### 2. Exploratory Analysis Workflow
- First load with pandas for column names and data types
- Then load with openpyxl for cell-level inspection (merged cells, formulas, formatting)
- This two-pass approach prevents blind modifications on incomplete understanding

### 3. Pre-Save Verification Checkpoint
- Before `wb.save()`, validate:
  - Target cell values match expectations
  - Row/column counts unchanged unless intentional
  - Merged cell status correct
- Print verification summaries to confirm all steps succeeded

### 4. Error Recovery from Library API Limitations
- When Excel library methods throw unexpected errors, search for alternative implementation patterns
- Example: `ws.unmerge_cells()` may require range string argument instead of no-argument call
- Test small code snippets incrementally to isolate API constraints
